title: Committed-secrets scan result: CLEAN (no secrets/PII)
evidence: gitleaks 0 leaks across 65 commits; builtin scanner 518 candidates all false-positive (508 high-entropy prose/hashes, 8 intentional author/test emails); saved secrets-scan.json
impact: No committed secret or leaked PII found

title: scan_secrets.py does not skip workflow-artifacts/ or *-lock.json (avoidable FP noise)
evidence: SKIP_DIR_NAMES (scan_secrets.py:94) lacks workflow-artifacts; SKIP_EXTS has .lock but not -lock.json; 91 of 518 candidates were in workflow-artifacts/ (incl. its own prior scan.json), 49 in package-lock.json
impact: Higher false-positive rate; scanner re-scans its own prior output on framework-using repos
recommended_action: Add workflow-artifacts to SKIP_DIR_NAMES and skip *-lock.json/*.lock.json generated lockfiles
validation: re-run scanner; count drops; add self-test

title: setup_tools.py lacks --version flag (inconsistent with the other 3 tools)
evidence: scan_secrets.py/run_checks.py/bench_env.py all have --version + _framework_version(); setup_tools.py main() (line 163) has only --format/--install
impact: Minor inconsistency; a user cannot query the framework version from this tool

title: scan_secrets.py computes shannon_entropy twice per high-entropy token
evidence: scan_secrets.py:204 (threshold check) and :209 (stored in extra) both call shannon_entropy(tok)
impact: Trivial redundant compute; no correctness impact
recommended_action: Compute once into a local and reuse
validation: code reads once; tests still pass

title: bench_env.capture_hpc() has no direct parse test (only diagnosis tested)
evidence: test_bench_env.py tests diagnose() with a hand-built hpc dict (test_hpc_login_node_flagged) but capture_hpc() scheduler detection is not directly exercised
impact: Low: detection is shutil.which-based; a regression in scheduler-name mapping would not be caught
recommended_action: Optional: add a test that monkeypatches which/env to assert schedulers/inside_allocation mapping

title: verify/run_checks.py cannot auto-discover this repo's own test command
evidence: run_checks.py --list discovers 0 checks; the test command lives only in CONTRIBUTING prose (python3 -m unittest discover -s tests -t .), not in package.json/Makefile/pyproject/tox/justfile that run_checks scans
impact: The framework does not dogfood its own verify discovery; a user running /verify here sees 'no runnable checks'
recommended_action: Add a minimal Makefile (or pyproject [tool] entry) with a `test` target so verify discovers it; low RR
source_files: run_checks.py --list; verify discovers the test target

title: ARCHITECTURE.md file-tree says shims are 'one per command, 15' - now 16 after benchmark
evidence: ARCHITECTURE.md:55 '.opencode/commands/ Generated OpenCode shims (one per command, 15)'; actual shim count is 16 (benchmark added, D41)
impact: Stale count; a reader cross-checking is misled; violates P2 honest docs

title: ARCHITECTURE.md says 'The three Python tools' + enumerates 3 - now four tools (bench_env added)
evidence: ARCHITECTURE.md:374-375 'The three Python tools (scan_secrets.py, setup_tools.py, run_checks.py) have stdlib-unittest self-tests'; bench_env.py is a 4th tool with 16 tests, omitted
impact: Stale count + incomplete enumeration; understates test coverage; violates P2
recommended_action: Update to four tools, add bench_env.py, mention its tests; also note the installer is tested

title: getting-started router omits a performance/benchmark goal
evidence: getting-started.md:49-61 enumerates 13 goals for every core workflow EXCEPT benchmark; a user asking 'how do I measure performance' is not routed
impact: Newcomer cannot discover the benchmark workflow via the tour (novice persona gap)
recommended_action: Add a route line: 'Measure/understand performance' -> benchmark

title: benchmark workflow not yet exercised end-to-end on a real repo
evidence: D41 added benchmark this session; bench_env.py has 16 unit tests but the guided body (authoring benchmarks/, HPC submission, results bundle) has not been run against a live target repo
impact: Low: the deterministic tool is tested; the guided prose flow is unproven in practice
recommended_action: Exercise /benchmark on a real repo (e.g. a CLI or an NFS-hosted project) before relying on it; this is validation, not a code fix - Remediation Risk of forcing a redesign now is Medium (functionality: premature changes without evidence)
